commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens trusted tool policy execution and before-tool-call diagnostics so malformed policy registrations, decisions, params, approvals, and policy ids fail closed or remain diagnosable while preserving valid no-op decision shapes.

PR surface: Source +352, Tests +358. Total +710 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main directly reads trusted-policy registrations, policy ids, and evaluation results; source inspection shows malformed getters or payloads can escape the policy path, and the PR adds focused tests for those cases.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human maintainer review is the blocker because this maintainer-labeled PR intentionally changes compatibility-sensitive trusted-policy fail-closed behavior.

target.title: fix(plugins): fail closed on trusted policy errors
target.body:
## Summary

- fails closed when trusted tool policy registration/list access, policy lookup, policy evaluation, decision fields, adjusted params, or approval payload basics are unreadable/malformed
- routes before-tool-call diagnostics through the same guarded trusted-policy reader so unreadable policy ids cannot crash diagnostic/native policy state
- preserves type-valid no-op results such as `{}` and `{ allow: undefined }` so compatibility hardening does not deny every tool call
- keeps this slice limited to trusted policy execution/diagnostics plus contract coverage with synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` fixtures only

## Verification

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts --reporter=verbose`: 2 routed shards, 113 tests passed on head `5215e6bb3bc`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main`
- touched-file/spec scrub for the reported plugin/tool names, too-close synthetic suffixes, private paths, and private network literals: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: first pass caught the `{}` no-op compatibility regression; fixed it; final pass clean
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`: AWS Crabbox run `run_f5d16063ce62`, lease `cbx_fb1e804b1c0b`, type `c7a.8xlarge`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`, `extensions`, `extensionTests`, exit 0 on base `8d6a6e9`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: trusted tool policy failures no longer fail open or crash policy evaluation/diagnostic state when policy registries, registrations, decisions, params, approval payloads, or policy ids are malformed/unreadable; malformed concrete decisions block deterministically while valid no-op decision shapes remain compatible.

Real environment tested: fresh linked OpenClaw `gwt` worktree from current `origin/main`, plus AWS Crabbox Linux remote changed gate for the final pruned head.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: audited the larger trusted-policy fuzz slice for over-hardening, pruned approval-option enum policing, preserved `{}` and `undefined` no-op compatibility, ran focused host-hooks contract and before-tool-call diagnostic tests, formatter check, diff check, scrub, autoreview until clean, and AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: contract coverage proves unreadable trusted policy lists/registrations/decisions/params/approval payloads and malformed concrete decisions return deterministic block results; agent diagnostics prove unreadable policy ids are reported through a fallback id instead of throwing; `{}` and optional `undefined` no-op decisions still pass through without denial.

Observed result after fix: a bad trusted-policy payload blocks only the unsafe tool call with a clear policy reason, and diagnostics can still describe the active policy set without crashing on unreadable policy metadata.

What was not tested: no live Telegram/UI round trip for this policy-only slice; runtime behavior is covered through plugin host-hook contract tests, before-tool-call diagnostic tests, autoreview, and the remote changed gate.
target.author: vincentkoc
